children.heading: Program established; purpose
children.label: a Program established; purpose
children.text: There is established the State Small Business Credit Initiative, to be administered by the Secretary. Under the Program, the Secretary shall allocate Federal funds to participating States and make the allocated funds available to the participating States as provided in this section for the uses described in this section.

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: Not later than 30 days after , the Secretary shall allocate Federal funds to participating States so that each State is eligible to receive an amount equal to what the State would receive under the 2021 allocation, as determined under paragraph (2).March 11, 2021

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: With respect to States other than Tribal governments, the Secretary shall determine the 2021 allocation by allocating Federal funds among the States in the proportion that each such State’s 2020 State employment decline bears to the aggregate of the 2020 State employment declines for all States.

children.heading: Minimum allocation
children.label: B Minimum allocation
children.text: The Secretary shall adjust the allocations under subparagraph (A) for each State to the extent necessary to ensure that no State receives less than 0.9 percent of the Federal funds.

children.heading: 2020 State employment decline defined1So in original. Two subpars. (C) have been enacted.1
children.label: C 2020 State employment decline defined1So in original. Two subpars. (C) have been enacted.1
i
children.text: the number of individuals employed in such State determined for December 2019; over

children.label: ii
children.text: the number of individuals employed in such State determined for December 2020.

children.heading: Separate allocation for Tribal governments1
children.label: C Separate allocation for Tribal governments1

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: With respect to States that are Tribal governments, the Secretary shall determine the 2021 allocation by allocating $500,000,000 among the Tribal governments in the proportion the Secretary determines appropriate, including with consideration to available employment and economic data regarding each such Tribal government.

children.heading: Notice of intent; timing of allocation
children.label: ii Notice of intent; timing of allocation
I
children.text: require Tribal governments that individually or jointly wish to participate in the Program to file a notice of intent with the Secretary not later than 30 days after ; andMarch 11, 2021

children.label: II
children.text: notwithstanding paragraph (1), allocate Federal funds to participating Tribal governments not later than 60 days after .March 11, 2021

children.heading: Employment data
children.label: D Employment data
children.text: If the Secretary determines that employment data with respect to a State is unavailable from the Bureau of Labor Statistics of the Department of Labor, the Secretary shall consider such other economic and employment data that is otherwise available for purposes of determining the employment data of such State.

children.heading: Allocated amount generally to be available to State in one-thirds
children.label: 1 Allocated amount generally to be available to State in one-thirds
i
children.text: apportion the participating State’s allocated amount into thirds;

children.label: ii
children.text: transfer to the participating State the first ⅓ when the Secretary approves the State for participation under ; andsection 5703 of this title

children.label: iii
children.text: transfer to the participating State each successive ⅓ when the State has certified to the Secretary that it has expended, transferred, or obligated 80 percent of the last transferred ⅓ for Federal contributions to, or for the account of, State programs that have delivered loans or investments to eligible businesses.

children.heading: Authority to withhold pending audit
children.label: B Authority to withhold pending audit
children.text: The Secretary may withhold the transfer of any successive ⅓ pending results of a financial audit.

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: The Inspector General of the Department of the Treasury shall carry out an audit of the participating State’s use of allocated Federal funds transferred to the State.

children.heading: Recoupment of misused transferred funds required
children.label: ii Recoupment of misused transferred funds required
children.text: The allocation agreement between the Secretary and the participating State shall provide that the Secretary shall recoup any allocated Federal funds transferred to the participating State if the results of the an audit include a finding that there was an intentional or reckless misuse of transferred funds by the State.

children.heading: Penalty for misstatement
children.label: iii Penalty for misstatement
children.text: Any participating State that is found to have intentionally misstated any report issued to the Secretary under the Program shall be ineligible to receive any additional funds under the Program. Funds that had been allocated or that would otherwise have been allocated to such participating State shall be paid into the general fund of the Treasury for reduction of the public debt.

children.heading: Municipalities
children.label: iv Municipalities
children.text: In this subparagraph, the term “participating State” shall include a municipality given special permission to participate in the Program, under .section 5703(d) of this title

children.heading: Exception
children.label: D Exception
children.text: The Secretary may, in the Secretary’s discretion, transfer the full amount of the participating State’s allocated amount to the State in a single transfer if the participating State applies to the Secretary for approval to use the full amount of the allocation as collateral for a qualifying loan or swap funding facility.

children.heading: Transferred amounts
children.label: 2 Transferred amounts
children.text: Each amount transferred to a participating State under this section shall remain available to the State until used by the State as permitted under paragraph (3).

children.label: A
children.text: for making Federal contributions to, or for the account of, an approved State program;

children.label: B
children.text: as collateral for a qualifying loan or swap funding facility;

children.label: C
children.text: in the case of the first ⅓ transferred, for paying administrative costs incurred by the State in implementing an approved State program in an amount not to exceed 5 percent of that first ⅓; or

children.label: D
children.text: in the case of each successive ⅓ transferred, for paying administrative costs incurred by the State in implementing an approved State program in an amount not to exceed 3 percent of that successive ⅓.

children.heading: Termination of availability of amounts not transferred
children.label: 4 Termination of availability of amounts not transferred
i
children.text: the second ⅓ of a State’s allocated amount has not been transferred to the State before the end of the end of the 3-year period beginning on the date that the Secretary approves the State for participation; or

children.label: ii
children.text: the last ⅓ of a State’s allocated amount has not been transferred to the State before the end of the end of the 6-year period beginning on the date that the Secretary approves the State for participation.

children.heading: Reallocation
children.label: B Reallocation
children.text: Any amount deemed by the Secretary to be no longer allocated to a State and no longer available to such State under subparagraph (A) may be reallocated by the Secretary to other participating States. In making such a reallocation, the Secretary shall not take into account the minimum allocation requirements under subsection (b)(2)(B) or the specific allocation for Tribal governments described under subsection (b)(2)(C).

children.heading: Transferred amounts not assistance
children.label: 5 Transferred amounts not assistance
children.text: The amounts transferred to a participating State under this section shall not be considered assistance for purposes of subtitle V of title 31.

children.label: A
children.text: the term “allocated amount” means the total amount of Federal funds allocated by the Secretary under subsection (b) to the participating State; and

children.label: i
children.text: in the case of the first ⅓ and second ⅓, an amount equal to 33 percent of a participating State’s allocated amount; and

children.label: ii
children.text: in the case of the last ⅓, an amount equal to 34 percent of a participating State’s allocated amount.

children.heading: Additional allocations to support business enterprises owned and controlled by socially and economically disadvantaged individuals
children.label: d Additional allocations to support business enterprises owned and controlled by socially and economically disadvantaged individuals

children.text: allocate $1,500,000,000 to States from funds allocated under this section and, by regulation or other guidance, prescribe Program requirements that the funds be expended for business enterprises owned and controlled by socially and economically disadvantaged individuals; and

allocate such amounts to States based on the needs of business enterprises owned and controlled by socially and economically disadvantaged individuals, as determined by the Secretary, in each State, and not subject to the allocation formula described under subsection (b).

children.heading: Incentive allocations to support business enterprises owned and controlled by socially and economically disadvantaged individuals
children.label: e Incentive allocations to support business enterprises owned and controlled by socially and economically disadvantaged individuals
children.text: Of the amounts appropriated for fiscal year 2021 to carry out the Program, the Secretary shall set aside $1,000,000,000 for an incentive program under which the Secretary shall increase the second ⅓ and last ⅓ allocations for States that demonstrate robust support, as determined by the Secretary, for business concerns owned and controlled by socially and economically disadvantaged individuals in the deployment of prior allocation amounts.

children.heading: Additional allocations to support very small businesses
children.label: f Additional allocations to support very small businesses

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: Of the amounts appropriated to carry out the Program, the Secretary shall allocate not less than $500,000,000 to States from funds allocated under this section to be expended for very small businesses.

children.label: A
children.text: means a business with fewer than 10 employees; and

children.label: B
children.text: may include independent contractors and sole proprietors.
